items.title: Girl's suspected abductor had been out of jail for just six days, police say

children.content: Northern Territory Police believe five-year-old Sharon Granites was abducted from a home in an Alice Springs town camp as the search continues into its third day.

articles.title: Criminal history of man police are searching for over missing 5yo girl revealed

children.content: A man being sought in connection with the alleged abduction of five-year-old Sharon Granites near Alice Springs has a long history of violence and prison time in the Northern Territory.

image.altText: A mugshot of an Aboriginal man, serious expression looking directly at camera, yellow / orange pigment behind brown eyes.

articles.title: Missing 5yo girl believed to have been abducted near Alice Springs, police say

children.content: Northern Territory Police say they believe a missing five-year-old girl in Alice Springs was abducted from her home at a town camp early on Sunday morning.

items.title: AI ball-tracking technology trialled in Darwin grade cricket

children.content: Darwin's premier women's cricket league becomes the first Australian grade competition to use an artificial intelligence umpire review system.

items.title: FOI documents show acting PM sought 'due diligence' on NT administrator

children.content: Months before a furore over his social media posts, documents show the federal government completed background checks on controversial NT Administrator David Connolly.

image.altText: A graphic shows a photo of David Connolly alongside a stack of paperwork.

items.title: Coalition points to stealth bombers as potential AUKUS stop-gap

children.content: The Coalition says Australia should consider buying next-generation stealth bombers to help bridge the gap until the AUKUS submarines arrive.

articles.title: Weapons maker Thales loses out on multi-billion-dollar missile contract

children.content: Thales was the favourite to win the contract to develop a mobile missile system for Australia, but now the future of its StrikeMaster missile launcher is uncertain.

items.title: Affidavit reveals more details as man charged with attempted Trump assassination

children.content: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

image.altText: man in hat and gown sits on concrete wall

items.title: Jimmy Kimmel says joke misconstrued as Trumps call to fire late-night host

children.content: Donald Trump says Jimmy Kimmel should be fired for a monologue he delivered days before the third alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

image.altText: Melania Trump looks straight ahead, while Pete Hegseth and Karoline Leavitt are seen beside her

items.title: A simple message of love: Memorial held at Port Arthur 30 years on from the massacre

children.content: Thirty years on from the Port Arthur tragedy, survivor Jane Scholefield had a simple message for those still bearing the scars of that day — keep love at the centre of mourning.

items.title: 'System … is broken': Mounjaro won't be listed on PBS in 'stand-off' with US maker

children.content: The "broken" process behind listing medication on the PBS contributes to hundreds of thousands of Australians with diabetes missing out on subsidised lifesaving medication, according to the peak industry body.

items.title: Electric ferry needs a lift as transport ship stuck in Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The world's largest electric ferry is sitting idle in Tasmania after construction was completed because the heavy-lift ship that was booked to take it to South America is stuck in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: ASX loses ground, Bank of Japan keeps rates at 0.75pc — as it happened

children.content: Australia's share market lost ground on Tuesday, and Brent crude oil futures rose back above $US100 a barrel, while the Bank of Japan kept its overnight rate at 0.75pc. Catch up on the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: NSW public transport Opal network is set to change. Here's what we know

items.title: Baby faces ongoing health concerns after birth delay in regional SA

items.title: Nedra Talley Ross of the Ronettes, who sang Baby I Love You, dies

children.content: Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the 1960s pop band the Ronettes, who sang the enduring hits Be My Baby, Baby I Love You, and Walking in the Rain alongside her cousins, has died aged 80.
